title: Protecting Your Information
description: Your company asks all employees to create strong passwords. Which is best?
choiceA: Use a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols.
choiceB: Use your birthdate for easy memory.
feedbackText: Strong passwords reduce the risk of unauthorized access.

title: Importance of Cybersecurity
description: Your manager says cybersecurity is only for IT. What should you do?
choiceA: Explain that everyone plays a role in keeping data safe.

title: Device Security
description: You notice your phone’s software is outdated.
choiceB: Ignore since it still works fine.

title: Public Wi-Fi
description: You’re at a café and connect to public Wi-Fi to access company files.
choiceA: Use a VPN for added protection.
feedbackText: VPNs encrypt data and protect you on unsecured networks.

title: Social Engineering
description: A friendly 'IT' person calls asking for your login credentials.
choiceB: Verify their identity before sharing anything.
feedbackText: Always verify identity; social engineering relies on trust.

title: Phishing Emails
description: You receive an email saying you won a prize. It asks you to click a link.
choiceA: Delete it or report to IT.
feedbackText: Phishing links steal information or install malware.

title: Malware Downloads
description: You find a 'free antivirus' online from an unknown site.
feedbackText: Malware often hides in fake software downloads.

title: Ransomware
description: Your files suddenly become locked, and a ransom note appears.
feedbackText: Paying doesn’t guarantee data recovery — contact IT.

title: Password Reuse
description: You use the same password for multiple sites.
choiceA: It’s fine if they’re all personal accounts.
feedbackText: Password reuse increases risk if one account is breached.

title: Fake Websites
description: A website URL looks strange but similar to your bank’s.
choiceA: Proceed if it looks close enough.
choiceB: Double-check the URL before entering credentials.
feedbackText: Always verify the URL and look for HTTPS.

title: USB Drive Risks
description: You find a USB labeled 'Confidential' in the office.
choiceA: Plug it in to see what’s inside.
feedbackText: Unknown USB drives can contain malware.

title: Insider Threats
description: A coworker asks for access to restricted files.
choiceA: Share them since they’re part of the team.
feedbackText: Only share data with authorized users.

title: Data Backup
description: How can you minimize damage from cyberattacks?
feedbackText: Backups help recovery after ransomware or data loss.

title: Suspicious Pop-ups
description: You see a browser pop-up claiming 'Your device is infected!'
feedbackText: Fake pop-ups trick users into installing malware.

title: Multi-Factor Authentication
description: You enable MFA for your work account.
choiceA: It adds an extra layer of protection.
choiceB: It’s unnecessary if your password is strong.
feedbackText: MFA helps prevent unauthorized access even if passwords are stolen.
